mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid drop in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a labor market rebound that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving immediate positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% from a 3-month prior level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the signal, or the signal fails to predict returns during the 2010-2015 period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based demand recovery, which sustains positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as corporate revenue visibility improves.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 60-day returns across three consecutive economic cycles, indicating decoupling of housing and labor from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion indicates resilient corporate capacity utilization and pricing power, supporting positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns in > 50% of observations during late-cycle phases, refuting the capacity utilization channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth above 2.0% triggers momentum buying as institutional investors rotate into cyclicals, generating positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth crosses the 2.0% threshold, or the signal generates negative returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the federal funds rate and CPI indicate aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint rise in rates and inflation fails to produce negative 60-day returns in > 50% of historical instances, or equity multiples expand despite the dual tightening signal.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic stability, which consistently supports positive equity returns over a monthly horizon through reduced risk premiums.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 10-year walk-forward test, or the signal flips direction during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales and food services revenue signals an immediate demand shock, leading to negative equity returns over the following week as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: Retail sales contractions greater than 3% are followed by positive or flat 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, indicating the signal captures noise rather than fundamental demand shifts.
